exemplar.title: To what extent is gender equality has been understand and applied among teenagers between 13 to 21 years old in daily life in Malaysia?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is gender equality has been understand and applied among teenagers between 13 to 21 years old in daily life in Malaysia?

strengths: Topic is communicated adequately with sufficient contextual explanation of gender equality among Malaysian teenagers
Introduction provides some policy and religious background that frames the investigation
A research question is present, indicating an attempt at focus
weaknesses: Research question is grammatically flawed, overly broad, and lacks clear operational definitions
Methodology relies solely on a single convenience-sample online survey without justification or triangulation
Parameters such as time frame, population descriptors, and sampling rationale are insufficiently defined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate an adequate communication of topic (band 3â4) balanced against an unclear, overly broad research question and a limited methodology (both band 1â2). On balance, the work sits at the lower end of the mid-range performance for Focus and Method, warranting 3/6.

strengths: Includes a substantial reference list demonstrating breadth of reading
Attempts to define key concepts such as gender equality and Islamic equity
Some tables and quantitative summaries (means, SD) provide tangible data application
weaknesses: Sources are often general websites with patchy in-text integration and minimal analysis
Subject-specific terminology is inconsistently applied and occasionally imprecise
Discussion of theoretical concepts is largely descriptive with little deeper insight or nuance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall clearly into the 1â2 band: limited use of sources, inconsistent terminology, and mostly descriptive content. This corresponds to 2/6 for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Data are correctly summarized with means and standard deviations
The evaluation section acknowledges sample-size limitations and demographic imbalance
A basic argument structure is present with ordered sections
weaknesses: Discussion remains superficial and descriptive, without critical engagement or synthesis of scholarly perspectives
No deep analysis linking results back to the research question or theoretical framework
Argumentation is loosely structured and transitions are weak, lacking coherent critical reasoning
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking strands are uniformly in the lowest band (1â3) due to minimal analytical depth, superficial discussion, and unclear argumentation. A score of 3/12 reflects the limited but present attempts at analysis and evaluation.

strengths: Clear use of standard section headings (Introduction, Methodology, etc.)
Inclusion of title page, word count, and basic visuals indicate awareness of layout requirements
weaknesses: No page numbers and inconsistent table/figure labelling hinder navigation
Referencing style is erratic and citation formatting is inconsistent
Sentence fragments and uneven paragraph structure disrupt overall coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation shows a recognisable structure and some layout elements (band 1â2) but suffers from missing page numbers, inconsistent labelling, and formatting errors. This performance aligns with 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: While sections are ordered, the argument largely strings together survey items without a coherent line of reasoning.  Transitions are weak and claims often lack evidential support, resulting in an unclear, loosely structured argument characteristic of the lowest assessed band (1â3).

Although a long reference list is appended, many citations are general websites, and in-text integration is patchy.  Sources are seldom analysed; instead they are used to restate background information.  This limited relevance and application corresponds to the 1â2 band.

Methodology is limited to a single on-line survey distributed by convenience sampling.  No rationale is offered for question design, sample size, or reliability checks, and there is no triangulation of methods.  While basic data handling (means, SD) is shown, the approach lacks depth and relevance beyond surface levelâconsistent with the lower (1â2) band description.

The student clearly states the broad theme of gender equality among Malaysian teenagers and provides some contextual explanation (religious diversity, policy background).  Nevertheless, the introduction contains repetitious material and grammatical flaws that hamper precision, and key parameters such as place, time-frame and population are only loosely sketched.  Overall, the topic is conveyed âadequately with sufficient explanation,â matching the 3â4 band.

Basic layout elementsâtitle page, word count and visualsâare included, but the document lacks page numbers and table/figure labelling is inconsistent.  Referencing style is erratic.  These partial fulfilments align with the 1â2 band for layout.

Analysis is restricted to computing means and standard deviations, followed by narrative summaries that restate the numbers.  There is little interpretation linking findings back to the research question or to theoretical frameworks.  As relevance is minimal and depth shallow, the work sits in the 1â3 band, nearer the middle.

Key terms (e.g., gender equality, *qiwÄmah*, equity) are mentioned, yet definitions are sometimes imprecise and usage is inconsistent.  Frequent grammatical errors further blur conceptual clarity.  Such partial command fits the lower band where terminology is "unclear and limited."

The âEvaluationâ section lists sample-size limitations and demographic imbalances but stops short of critically weighing evidence or offering well-supported conclusions.  Discussion remains surface-level and occasionally speculative, matching the descriptors for limited and superficial discussion in the 1â3 band.

Headings (Introduction, Methodology, etc.) provide a recognisable framework, yet paragraphs are uneven and the sequence sometimes repeats background information.  The structure, though present, shows notable weaknesses, corresponding to the 1â2 band.

A research question is provided, yet it is grammatically incorrect and simultaneously asks about both âunderstandingâ and âapplication,â making the scope too wide for an EE.  The phrasing does not define what indicators will measure those constructs, so focus remains hazy.  This aligns with the 1â2 band where the question is stated but unclear and overly broad.

The essay recounts Islamic concepts such as *adl*, *qist* and *qiwÄmah* and references Malaysian policy documents, but treatment is largely descriptive and occasionally inaccurate (e.g., conflating equity and equality without nuance).  There is little demonstration of deeper theoretical insight, placing the work in the âlimited and mostly descriptiveâ (1â2) band.

annotations.comment: In-text citations alternate formats (e.g., APA parentheses vs. narrative style) creating an inconsistent referencing approach that should be standardized.
The introduction provides general context but repeats background information and omits essential scope details such as specific time frame, precise location, and defined population parameters.

The research question is phrased incorrectly (âis gender equality has beenâ¦â); it conflates understanding and application and lacks defined indicators, leading to an overly broad and unfocused inquiry.
A sentence fragment beginning âWhich is due toâ¦â disrupts flow and clarity; please integrate it into the previous sentence to maintain coherence and formal style.
The document lacks a clear title of the investigation at the start; the studentâs name appears instead of an investigation title, failing a key formatting requirement.
Page numbering is absent, making navigation and citation of specific sections difficult; please add consistent page numbers throughout.
Subject-verb agreement error (âIslamic teachings highlightsâ) undermines credibility; revise to âIslamic teachings highlightâ for grammatical accuracy and consistency in terminology.
The evaluation section acknowledges sample size and religious imbalance but does not critically assess how these limitations may systematically bias results.
The evaluation heading appears immediately before the conclusion, but formal structure typically distinguishes âDiscussionâ and âEvaluationâ as separate sections prior to âConclusion.â
The conclusion summarises findings but does not explicitly answer how religious teachings and cultural norms interact to shape teenagersâ gender equality practices as stated in the research aim.
There is no author reflection on the methodological decisions (e.g., choice of convenience sampling), omitting engagement with the research process and potential researcher bias.
The Google Sheets screenshot shows raw responses but is not integrated into the narrative or summarized; summarize key patterns instead of pasting full data views.
Including a table of means and standard deviations offers an effective quantitative overview of central tendencies across all Likert items.
The discussion remains largely descriptive, restating source content without critically engaging or synthesizing differing scholarly perspectives on Islamic equity and cultural practice.
Elaborate cultural commentary within Q8 analysis shifts focus from survey data to broad religious practices without relating back to the respondentsâ perspectives.
The Q11 discussion ends mid-sentence (âvisible toâ), indicating an unfinished thought and lack of thorough proofreading.
The problem statement introduces leadership stereotypes but does not explicitly link back to the research question or explain how these stereotypes will be examined, reducing its focus and relevance.
The methodology section lacks rationale for the chosen survey design, sample size, reliability checks, or ethical considerations, limiting its methodological rigor and transparency.
Table 1 (Q1) is uncaptioned and unlabeled; please provide a descriptive title and context for readers to understand its purpose within the methodology.
The extensive table of Q2âQ14 spans the full page without a clear table number or concise caption, making it difficult to reference and interpret within the text.
The Q15âQ23 table repeats earlier formatting issues: it lacks numbering, a concise title, and splits across lines, impairing readability and cohesion.
Including raw screenshots of the Google Forms without explaining ethical clearance for image use or data privacy considerations distracts and raises potential consent concerns.
There is no discussion of survey piloting or validation of the questionnaire items; consider adding steps taken to ensure instrument reliability and validity.

Survey distribution is described only as âsharedâ¦ as much as possible,â without indicating sampling frame, recruitment strategy, or response rate calculationsâdetails essential for methodological clarity.
The narrative introducing the Q4âQ23 table does not explain how these items directly relate to the research question or theoretical framework.
The bar chart is an accurate visualization but the text merely restates numerical values without deeper interpretation or connection to broader theoretical perspectives.
Analysis of Q4 reports mean and SD correctly but lacks inferential statistics or thresholds to assess whether the uniform response is statistically meaningful.
Discussion of Q5 introduces Buddhist and Hindu examples abruptly, without clear linkage to the Malaysian teenage sample or the data collected.
Headings for Q8 use underlining and h2 elements inconsistently relative to Q7 and Q9, disrupting structural hierarchy and reader navigation.
Figures labeled âDiagram 1â through â3â are placed without textual references; each must be cited in the body before or after its placement for reader orientation.
The pie chart shows 98.2% Muslim respondents but no discussion of the implications for sample representativeness or potential cultural bias in interpreting results.
The text refers to the Malaysia Education Blueprint 2013â2025 while the reference list cites the 2015â2025 edition, creating a discrepancy.
The references list exhibits inconsistent formatting (styles, punctuation, italics) and incomplete publisher details; adopt a single citation style and apply it uniformly.
